rationale: The ASMR-satisfaction pour aesthetic aligns well with Marble Sort's calm/satisfying mood and the 25-55 female casual audience that drives organic engagement on sorting/satisfaction content. However, there are real frictions: the archetype's palette (pink/blue/grey) clashes with the game's lavender+orange identity, and a "pouring flakes into a labeled jar" hook doesn't naturally telegraph the tap-to-release + conveyor + color-box mechanic — viewers may not connect the ad to the actual gameplay. Recommend testing an adapted version where marbles (not flakes) pour from trays into color-labeled boxes, keeping the ASMR pacing but swapping in the game's palette and showing the conveyor reveal in seconds 2-3 to fix the mechanic mismatch; also note the cluster is thin (1 creative, 1 advertiser) so signal confidence is low.
